from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a natural language description from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Mapping predicates to phrase generators
    phrasing = {
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is also known as {o}",
        "creator": lambda s, o: f"{s} was created by {o}",
        "fullName": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s full name is {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded by {o}",
        "keyPerson": lambda s, o: f"{s} has key person {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "product": lambda s, o: f"{s} produces {o}",
        "lastAired": lambda s, o: f"{s} last aired on {o}",
        "starring": lambda s, o: f"{s} stars {o}",
        "firstAppearanceInFilm": lambda s, o: f"{s} first appeared in the film {o}",
        "voice": lambda s, o: f"{s} is voiced by {o}",
        "distributor": lambda s, o: f"{o} distributed {s}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o}",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the {o}",
        "broadcastedBy": lambda s, o: f"{s} was broadcasted by {o}",
        "firstAired": lambda s, o: f"{s} first aired on {o}",
        "series": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the series {o}",
        "child": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a child named {o}",
    }
    # Helper to remove surrounding quotes from object values
    def _clean(obj: str) -> str:
        return obj.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
    # Build clause list
    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        func = phrasing.get(triple.predicate)
        obj = _clean(triple.object)
        if func:
            # many phrase functions expect raw object; re‑apply cleaning inside if needed
            clause = func(triple.subject, obj)
        else:
            clause = f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {obj}"
        clauses.append(clause)
    # Combine clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        return clauses[0].rstrip() + "."
    # join with commas and an 'and' before the last clause
    combined = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]
    return combined.rstrip() + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
